source_excerpt_max_25_words: provide a description in the form of metadata of the available datasets and their characteristics.
addressee_legal_role: Health data access body responsible for the publicly available dataset catalogue.
action: Provide a metadata description of each available dataset and its characteristics through the specified catalogue.
object_granularity: One metadata description linked to one available dataset.
purpose: Make datasets available for secondary use discoverable through a consistent machine-readable catalogue.
trigger_conditions: An electronic-health dataset is available within the health data access body's Chapter IV catalogue responsibility.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Publicly available catalogue AND standardised catalogue AND machine-readable catalogue AND a metadata description of the dataset and its characteristics.
exceptions_derogations: Dataset scope remains bounded by Articles 50 and 51; the mandatory content of each description is separately recorded in REQ-EHDS-077-006.
required_information: Dataset identity, availability and characteristics sufficient to link the catalogue description; mandatory content fields are governed by REQ-EHDS-077-006.
required_form_or_format: Metadata in a publicly available, standardised, machine-readable dataset catalogue.
required_control: Verify catalogue accessibility, standardisation, machine readability, dataset coverage and description linkage.
required_evidence: Resolvable catalogue record, holder-supplied description, publication endpoint and current version evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The dataset descriptions in the national dataset catalogue shall be available in at least one official language of the Union.
addressee_legal_role: Health data access body operating a national dataset catalogue.
action: Make each national-catalogue dataset description available in at least one official Union language.
object_granularity: Each dataset description in one national dataset catalogue.
purpose: Make national catalogue descriptions linguistically accessible.
trigger_conditions: A national dataset catalogue contains a dataset description.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: National catalogue classification AND each description available in one or more official Union languages.
exceptions_derogations: The all-official-languages rule for the Union-institution catalogue is separately recorded in REQ-EHDS-077-007.
required_information: Catalogue type, description identifier, selected official Union language and version.
required_form_or_format: At least one official Union language per national-catalogue description.
required_control: Verify catalogue type, official-language status, description coverage, accessibility and version synchronisation.
required_evidence: Accessible language version, locale identifier and publication record for each description.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The dataset catalogue shall be made available to single information points established or designated under Article 8 of Regulation (EU) 2022/868.
addressee_legal_role: Operator responsible for the relevant EHDS dataset catalogue.
action: Make the dataset catalogue available to the designated single information points.
object_granularity: Catalogue-level connection or availability arrangement.
purpose: Connect EHDS dataset discoverability with the Data Governance Act information-point framework.
trigger_conditions: A relevant single information point has been established or designated under Regulation (EU) 2022/868 Article 8.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Identity of the designated point AND availability of the catalogue endpoint or equivalent access arrangement.
exceptions_derogations: No express derogation identified; implementation method is not specified by Article 77(3).
required_information: Catalogue endpoint, responsible bodies and connection or access details.
required_form_or_format: Article 77(3) does not prescribe a specific interface; the catalogue itself remains standardised and machine-readable under Article 77(1).
required_control: Designation verification, endpoint availability test and responsibility register.
required_evidence: Designation record, technical endpoint or access agreement, and availability test.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: By 26 March 2027, the Commission shall, by means of implementing acts, set out the minimum elements health data holders are to provide
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority under EHDS Article 77(4).
action: Adopt implementing rules for minimum dataset elements and their characteristics.
object_granularity: Article 77(4) implementing act and each specified minimum element.
purpose: Harmonise the information health data holders supply for dataset descriptions.
trigger_conditions: The Article 77(4) legislative mandate and deadline govern Commission implementation.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Implementing act AND minimum elements AND characteristics of those elements.
exceptions_derogations: The act's operational application follows Article 105; the deadline to set the rules does not itself activate all Chapter IV holder duties.
required_information: Minimum elements health data holders are to provide and the characteristics of each element.
required_control: Deadline register, legal-basis check, completeness of minimum-element specification and source-status monitoring.
required_evidence: Adopted and published implementing act with Article 77(4) legal basis.
consequence_or_remedy: Missed deadline or defective legal basis requires institutional and legal review; no automatic substitute specification is inferred.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2).
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority using the EHDS examination procedure.
action: Use the Article 98(2) examination procedure when adopting the dataset-element implementing act.
object_granularity: Adoption procedure for each Article 77(4) implementing act.
purpose: Subject the implementing measure to the prescribed Member-State committee control.
trigger_conditions: The Commission proceeds to adopt an implementing act under Article 77(4).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Article 98(2) incorporation AND Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5 examination steps.
exceptions_derogations: No alternative adoption procedure is provided in Article 77(4).
required_information: Legal basis, committee opinion, voting outcome and adopted act.
required_form_or_format: Examination procedure under Article 98(2) and Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5.
required_control: Procedure checklist and committee-record verification.
required_evidence: Committee dossier, opinion or voting record, adopted text and publication record.
consequence_or_remedy: A procedural defect can affect validity and requires qualified legal assessment.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The description of each dataset shall include information concerning the source, scope, main characteristics, and nature of the electronic health data in the dataset
addressee_legal_role: Health data access body providing the dataset description through the catalogue.
action: Ensure that each dataset description includes every information category specified in Article 77(1).
object_granularity: One metadata description for one dataset, checked across all five required information categories.
purpose: Enable informed dataset discovery and assessment of relevance and availability conditions.
trigger_conditions: A dataset description is provided under REQ-EHDS-077-001.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Source AND scope AND main characteristics AND nature of the electronic health data AND conditions for making those data available.
exceptions_derogations: No express omission or alternative-content derogation appears in Article 77(1).
required_information: Dataset source; scope; main characteristics; nature of electronic health data; conditions for making the data available.
required_form_or_format: Structured metadata fields within the catalogue description.
required_control: Per-description completeness, provenance, validation and maintenance controls for every required information category.
required_evidence: Catalogue record, field-level metadata, holder-supplied description, provenance and source-status evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: An incomplete description fails the Article 77(1) content duty; any sanction depends on Article 99 and applicable national rules.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The dataset catalogue for Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies provided by the Union health data access service shall be available in all official languages
addressee_legal_role: Commission acting through or responsible for the Union health data access service that provides the Union-institution catalogue.
action: Make the Union-institution dataset catalogue available in all official Union languages.
object_granularity: The complete catalogue for Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies.
purpose: Provide Union-wide linguistic access to the Union-institution dataset catalogue.
trigger_conditions: The Union health data access service provides the catalogue for Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Union-institution catalogue classification AND availability in every official Union language.
exceptions_derogations: The at-least-one-language rule for national descriptions is separately recorded in REQ-EHDS-077-002.
required_information: Catalogue identity, authoritative official-language list, translated content and synchronised versions.
required_form_or_format: The catalogue must be available in all official languages of the Union.
required_control: Verify catalogue classification, complete language coverage, accessibility, translation integrity and version synchronisation.
required_evidence: Accessible catalogue versions, locale identifiers, translation records and publication evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: Missing an official-language version leaves the all-languages availability duty unsatisfied.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Datasets made available through health data access bodies may have a Union data quality and utility label applied by the health data holders.
addressee_legal_role: Holder of a dataset made available through a health data access body.
action: Apply a Union data quality and utility label to the dataset.
object_granularity: One label for one dataset.
purpose: Provide transparent quality and utility information supporting informed dataset selection.
trigger_conditions: A dataset is made available through a health data access body and paragraph 2 does not independently make the label mandatory.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Voluntary permission under paragraph 1; mandatory branch under paragraph 2 if public-funding facts are met.
exceptions_derogations: The label does not determine whether a dataset may be made available and does not replace a data permit or request approval.
required_information: When used, the label must cover applicable Article 78(3) elements.
required_form_or_format: Union data quality and utility label; final visual and technical specifications depend on Article 78(6).
required_control: Confirm voluntary versus mandatory branch and verify label completeness and supporting documentation.
required_evidence: Holder decision, label, linked dataset and supporting documentation.
consequence_or_remedy: No adverse inference follows from choosing not to label under paragraph 1 alone; paragraph 2 may separately impose a duty.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Datasets with electronic health data collected and processed with the support of Union or national public funding shall have a data quality and utility label
addressee_legal_role: Holder responsible for a publicly supported electronic-health dataset.
action: Apply a complete data quality and utility label to the dataset.
object_granularity: One label for each dataset meeting the funding trigger.
purpose: Ensure publicly supported electronic-health datasets disclose comparable quality and utility information.
trigger_conditions: Electronic health data in the dataset were collected and processed with support of Union or national public funding.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Union OR national public funding; collection and processing support must be established; label covers the applicable paragraph 3 elements.
exceptions_derogations: Where applicable qualifies individual paragraph 3 elements, not the paragraph 2 public-funding trigger itself.
required_information: Funding provenance plus every applicable Article 78(3)(a)-(f) element.
required_form_or_format: Data quality and utility label conforming to applicable Article 78(6) specifications.
required_control: Funding-source classification, evidence of support across collection and processing, and label completeness check.
required_evidence: Grant, procurement or funding records; label; supporting documentation supplied under Article 60(4).
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: metadata, support documentation, the data dictionary, the format and standards used, the source of the data and, where applicable, the data model
addressee_legal_role: Holder applying a data quality and utility label.
action: Include the required data-documentation information in the label.
object_granularity: Data-documentation component of one dataset label.
purpose: Expose the documentation and semantic context needed to understand and assess a dataset.
trigger_conditions: A label is applied and the identified documentation element exists or is applicable to the dataset.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Metadata, support documentation, data dictionary, format, standards and source are listed; data model is conditional where applicable.
exceptions_derogations: Where applicable requires a reasoned per-element determination; it is not a blanket omission permission.
required_information: Metadata; support documentation; data dictionary; format; standards used; data source; applicable data model.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component linked to supporting documentation under the applicable technical specification.
required_control: Element-by-element presence, applicability and traceability check.
required_evidence: Label fields and resolvable supporting documents.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the completeness, uniqueness, accuracy, validity, timeliness and consistency of the data
addressee_legal_role: Health data holder applying the data quality and utility label.
action: Report the listed technical-quality dimensions in the label.
object_granularity: Technical-quality component of one dataset label.
purpose: Allow users and access bodies to assess technical data quality.
trigger_conditions: A data quality and utility label is applied or required under Article 78(1) or (2), and the Article 78(3)(b) technical-quality element is applicable.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Completeness AND uniqueness AND accuracy AND validity AND timeliness AND consistency.
exceptions_derogations: No express exception; Article 78(3) applies the listed elements where applicable.
required_information: Completeness; uniqueness; accuracy; validity; timeliness; consistency.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component with metrics or classifications required by the applicable technical specification.
required_control: Metric definition, calculation provenance, measurement date and dataset-version linkage.
required_evidence: Quality reports, calculation outputs and label values.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the level of maturity of the data quality management processes, including review and audit processes, and bias examination
addressee_legal_role: Holder applying a data quality and utility label.
action: Report data-quality-management maturity and the listed assurance processes.
object_granularity: Quality-management-process component of one dataset label.
purpose: Expose governance maturity and assurance practices behind dataset quality claims.
trigger_conditions: A label is applied and quality-management, review, audit or bias practices are applicable.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Maturity level plus review and audit processes plus bias examination, subject to reasoned applicability.
exceptions_derogations: Article 78 does not define a maturity scale; later specifications or adopted standards may control representation.
required_information: Maturity level; review process; audit process; bias-examination process.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component with supporting assurance documentation.
required_control: Governance maturity assessment, evidence links, review cadence and bias-examination accountability.
required_evidence: Quality-management procedures, review and audit records, bias-examination record and label value.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the period, population coverage and, where applicable, representativity of the population sampled, and the average timeframe in which a natural person appears in a dataset
addressee_legal_role: Holder applying a data quality and utility label.
action: Report the listed temporal and population-coverage characteristics.
object_granularity: Coverage component of one dataset label.
purpose: Allow assessment of temporal scope, population coverage and potential representativity.
trigger_conditions: A label is applied; representativity and person-duration measures are included where applicable.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Period and population coverage; sample representativity where applicable; average person-presence timeframe where meaningful.
exceptions_derogations: Representativity is expressly conditional; methodology and benchmark population are not fixed in the article.
required_information: Coverage period; population coverage; applicable representativity; average timeframe a natural person appears.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component with population and temporal definitions.
required_control: Reference-population, sampling, time-window and calculation-method documentation.
required_evidence: Coverage analysis, sampling documentation, calculation outputs and label values.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the time between the collection of the electronic health data and their addition to the dataset and the time needed to provide electronic health data
addressee_legal_role: Holder applying a data quality and utility label with access and provision information.
action: Report the two specified data-latency measures.
object_granularity: Access-and-provision component of one dataset label.
purpose: Communicate data freshness and expected provision timing.
trigger_conditions: A label is applied and the relevant collection, addition, permit or request-approval events can be defined.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Collection-to-addition time AND post-permit or post-request-approval provision time, where applicable to the route.
exceptions_derogations: The data permit and health-data-request approval routes are alternatives; Article 78(3)(e) does not itself set service-level thresholds.
required_information: Collection date or period; dataset-addition date; issue or approval event; time needed to provide data.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component with defined units, start events and end events.
required_control: Event-definition, clock-start, clock-stop, route and statistic consistency checks.
required_evidence: Timestamp records, permit or approval record, provision logs and label values.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: merging and adding data to an existing dataset, including links with other datasets
addressee_legal_role: Holder applying a data quality and utility label.
action: Report specified dataset modifications and inter-dataset links.
object_granularity: Modification component of one dataset label.
purpose: Expose provenance-affecting changes and linkages that influence interpretation and utility.
trigger_conditions: A label is applied and the dataset has been merged, augmented or linked.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Disclose each applicable merging, addition and inter-dataset link event.
exceptions_derogations: No disclosure is required for a modification type that did not occur; protected operational detail may need lawful abstraction rather than omission.
required_information: Merge events; additions; links to other datasets; affected versions and dates.
required_form_or_format: Article 78 label component linked to provenance or change documentation.
required_control: Transformation and version lineage with link integrity.
required_evidence: Change logs, provenance records, dataset identifiers and label values.
consequence_or_remedy: Potential supervision and national penalties depend on Article 99 and the competent Member State's implementing rules; no specific sanction is inferred here.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: it shall assess whether the dataset covered by the label meets the quality requirements forming part of the elements
addressee_legal_role: Access body responsible for the dataset carrying the questioned label.
action: Assess whether the labelled dataset meets the relevant quality requirements.
object_granularity: One questioned label and the dataset it covers.
purpose: Protect the accuracy and trustworthiness of Union data quality and utility labels.
trigger_conditions: The health data access body has reason to believe a label might be inaccurate.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Reasoned suspicion AND assessment against the quality requirements forming part of applicable paragraph 3 elements.
exceptions_derogations: Mere possibility without a reasoned basis does not establish the trigger; Article 60(4) holder documentation supports verification.
required_information: Grounds for concern; applicable label elements; quality requirements; dataset and label versions.
required_form_or_format: Documented assessment capable of supporting the revocation decision.
required_control: Trigger triage, evidence request, requirement-by-requirement assessment and conflict-of-interest control.
required_evidence: Reason-for-belief record, Article 60(4) documentation, assessment record and finding.
consequence_or_remedy: A finding of non-conformity triggers the separate mandatory revocation clause in REQ-EHDS-078-010.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: in the event the dataset does not meet the quality requirements, shall revoke the label.
addressee_legal_role: Access body that assessed the questioned label.
action: Revoke the data quality and utility label.
object_granularity: One label found unsupported by its dataset's quality requirements.
purpose: Remove an inaccurate or unsupported quality signal from circulation.
trigger_conditions: Completed Article 78(4) assessment finds that the dataset does not meet the relevant quality requirements.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Competent assessment AND negative finding; revocation follows rather than an unrecorded label downgrade.
exceptions_derogations: No discretion to retain the label after the stated negative finding is expressed in the clause.
required_information: Label identifier, dataset identifier, failed requirements, finding date and revocation status.
required_form_or_format: Recorded revocation propagated to catalogue and downstream label displays.
required_control: Decision authorisation, versioned status change, notification and propagation checks.
required_evidence: Assessment finding, revocation decision, catalogue update and audit log.
consequence_or_remedy: Mandatory label revocation; any further penalty depends on Article 99 and national law.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend this Regulation
addressee_legal_role: Holder of the Article 78(5) delegated power.
action: Adopt a delegated act modifying, adding or removing label elements.
object_granularity: Delegated act and each affected Article 78(3) element.
purpose: Keep the label-element framework adaptable through controlled delegated legislation.
trigger_conditions: The Commission elects to exercise the Article 78(5) power within Article 97's delegation and procedure.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Modify OR add OR remove elements; every route remains subject to Article 97.
exceptions_derogations: A proposal, draft or consultation is not a binding amendment; only a valid adopted and effective delegated act changes the element set.
required_information: Affected element, amendment rationale, consultation record, notification and effective date.
required_control: Delegation-validity, expert-consultation, notification, scrutiny and effective-date checks.
required_evidence: Adopted delegated act, Article 97 procedural record, publication and entry-into-force evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: An effective delegated act changes the controlling element set; invalid or non-effective material must not be used as binding law.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: By 26 March 2027, the Commission shall, by means of implementing acts, set out the visual characteristics and technical specifications
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority under EHDS Article 78(6).
action: Adopt visual and technical specifications for the data quality and utility label.
object_granularity: Article 78(6) implementing act and each visual or technical specification.
purpose: Provide a harmonised, implementable label presentation and technical model.
trigger_conditions: The Article 78(6) legislative mandate and deadline govern Commission implementation.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Visual characteristics AND technical specifications, based on the paragraph 3 element set.
exceptions_derogations: Operational use follows Article 105's staged dates; draft specifications are not binding.
required_information: Visual characteristics; technical specifications; linkage to every paragraph 3 element.
required_control: Deadline, legal basis, specification completeness, version and source-status monitoring.
required_evidence: Adopted and published implementing act with Article 78(6) legal basis.
consequence_or_remedy: Missed deadline or defective legal basis requires institutional and legal review; no substitute format is inferred.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2) of this Regulation.
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority using the EHDS examination procedure.
action: Use the Article 98(2) examination procedure when adopting label specifications.
object_granularity: Adoption procedure for each Article 78(6) implementing act.
purpose: Subject the implementing measure to prescribed Member-State committee control.
trigger_conditions: The Commission proceeds to adopt an implementing act under Article 78(6).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Article 98(2) incorporation AND Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5 examination steps.
exceptions_derogations: No alternative adoption procedure is provided in Article 78(6).
required_information: Legal basis, committee opinion, voting outcome and adopted act.
required_form_or_format: Examination procedure under Article 98(2) and Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5.
required_control: Procedure checklist and committee-record verification.
required_evidence: Committee dossier, opinion or voting record, adopted text and publication record.
consequence_or_remedy: A procedural defect can affect validity and requires qualified legal assessment.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: take into account the requirements in Article 10 of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and any adopted common specifications or harmonised standards
addressee_legal_role: Commission drafter of the Article 78(6) label implementing act.
action: Identify, assess and take account of relevant AI Act requirements and formally adopted supporting specifications or standards.
object_granularity: Cross-instrument basis for each relevant label specification.
purpose: Align EHDS label specifications with the AI Act data-governance framework where relevant.
trigger_conditions: The Commission prepares Article 78(6) specifications and an AI Act Article 10 requirement, adopted common specification or harmonised standard is relevant.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Always consider Article 10 requirements; consider adopted supporting common specifications OR harmonised standards where applicable and formally operative.
exceptions_derogations: Drafts, technical reports and uncited standards must not be treated as adopted common specifications or presumption-conferring harmonised standards.
required_information: Relevant Article 10 requirement; formal status, version, scope and coverage of each common specification or harmonised standard.
required_form_or_format: Documented consideration in the implementing-act evidence base and resulting specification.
required_control: Source-status check, formal-status verification, coverage matrix and rationale for inclusion or non-applicability.
required_evidence: Authoritative AI Act text; adopted common-specification act or qualifying Official Journal citation; drafting record.
consequence_or_remedy: Failure to take required considerations into account may affect the legal robustness of the implementing act and needs qualified review.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the dataset content, use restrictions, licences, data collection methodology, data quality and uncertainty shall be sufficiently described
addressee_legal_role: Participant offering data or data services to another data-space participant.
action: Create and provide a sufficiently detailed discovery and use description.
object_granularity: One dataset or data-service offering.
purpose: Allow the recipient to find, access and use the data.
trigger_conditions: A covered participant offers data or data services to another participant.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Describe content, restrictions, licences, collection methodology, quality and uncertainty; use machine-readable format where applicable; description must enable find, access and use.
exceptions_derogations: Where applicable qualifies machine readability and context-specific fields; other Union or national law remains controlling.
required_information: Dataset content; use restrictions; licences; collection methodology; data quality; uncertainty.
required_control: Offering-scope, field-completeness, machine-readability and recipient-task sufficiency review.
required_evidence: Published description, machine-readable representation where applicable, and recipient access/use test evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance may engage the Data Act competent-authority and Article 40 penalty framework; the exact measure requires national-law and case verification.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the data structures, data formats, vocabularies, classification schemes, taxonomies and code lists, where available, shall be described in a publicly available and consistent manner
addressee_legal_role: Participant offering data or data services to another data-space participant.
action: Publish consistent descriptions of the listed structural and semantic resources.
object_granularity: Offering-level structure or semantic resource.
purpose: Enable syntactic and semantic interoperability.
trigger_conditions: A covered offering uses or has available any listed structure, format or semantic resource.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: For each available resource, public availability AND consistency of its description.
exceptions_derogations: Where available limits the listed resource duty but requires a reasoned availability determination; it does not waive consistency for resources that exist.
required_information: Data structures; formats; vocabularies; classification schemes; taxonomies; code lists; versions and identifiers.
required_control: Resource inventory, canonical identifier, version alignment, link resolution and internal consistency checks.
required_evidence: Published schemas, vocabularies, code lists or reasoned not-available record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance may engage the Data Act competent-authority and Article 40 penalty framework; the exact measure requires national-law and case verification.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: technical means to access the data, such as application programming interfaces, and their terms of use and quality of service shall be sufficiently described
addressee_legal_role: Participant offering data or data services to another data-space participant.
action: Describe technical access means, terms of use and quality of service to enable automated access and transmission.
object_granularity: One access interface or technical means for an offering.
purpose: Enable automatic inter-party access and transmission, including continuous, bulk or real-time modes where lawful and feasible.
trigger_conditions: A covered participant offers data or data services and provides a technical access means.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Sufficiently describe access means, terms and service quality; enable automatic machine-readable access/transmission where technically feasible and without hampering connected-product functioning.
exceptions_derogations: Automatic mode is limited by technical feasibility and non-interference with connected-product good functioning; other applicable law remains controlling.
required_information: Access means or API; terms of use; quality of service; supported continuity, bulk or real-time modes; machine-readable representations; limitations.
required_form_or_format: Sufficient technical description enabling automation; machine-readable transmission where feasible.
required_control: Interface documentation, terms-version control, service-level monitoring and feasibility/non-interference assessment.
required_evidence: Published API or access documentation, terms, service metrics and automated transfer test.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance may engage the Data Act competent-authority and Article 40 penalty framework; the exact measure requires national-law and case verification.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the means to enable the interoperability of tools for automating the execution of data sharing agreements, such as smart contracts shall be provided
addressee_legal_role: Participant in a data space offering data or data services to other participants.
action: Provide interoperability means for automated data-sharing-agreement tools.
object_granularity: Tool, protocol or interface supporting one or more data-sharing agreements.
purpose: Allow interoperable automated execution of data-sharing agreements.
trigger_conditions: The participant offers data or data services to other participants in a data space.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: The participant offers data or data services AND Article 33 applies; where applicable, means enabling interoperability of agreement-automation tools are provided.
exceptions_derogations: No express exception; 'where applicable' qualifies the means required by point (d), and smart contracts remain an example rather than the exclusive technology.
required_information: Supported automation tools, protocols, identifiers, semantic rules, execution conditions and interface constraints.
required_form_or_format: Interoperable technical means; the article does not prescribe one implementation.
required_control: Tool-scope assessment, conformance test, agreement-state traceability and failure handling.
required_evidence: Interface or protocol specification, executable test and agreement execution record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance may engage the Data Act competent-authority and Article 40 penalty framework; the exact measure requires national-law and case verification.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: while taking fully into account the interrelation with requirements arising from other Union or national law.
addressee_legal_role: Participant, specification authority or decision-maker applying Article 33 requirements.
action: Qualify the formulation, application or assessment of Article 33 requirements by accounting for their interrelation with other Union or national law.
object_granularity: Requirement, sectoral profile or covered implementation.
purpose: Prevent Article 33 implementation from ignoring controlling sectoral or other legal requirements.
trigger_conditions: An Article 33 requirement is implemented, further specified, standardised or assessed.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Generic OR sector-specific requirement design; in either case, fully account for applicable Union and national law.
exceptions_derogations: The clause does not itself resolve conflicts or disapply other law.
required_information: Applicable Union and national requirements, conflict or overlap analysis, sectoral scope and chosen implementation.
required_form_or_format: Documented cross-law analysis; no particular technical format is prescribed.
required_control: Applicable-law inventory, conflict check, sectoral profile governance and change monitoring.
required_evidence: Legal analysis, source register, profile decision and traceable implementation rationale.
consequence_or_remedy: The clause does not itself resolve a conflict, establish a hierarchy, disapply another rule or determine invalidity or non-compliance; any legal effect must be assessed under the applicable interacting rules.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts, in accordance with Article 45 of this Regulation to supplement this Regulation
addressee_legal_role: Holder of the Article 33(2) delegated power.
action: Adopt a delegated act further specifying eligible Article 33(1) requirements.
object_granularity: Delegated act and each further-specified essential requirement.
purpose: Make otherwise ineffective essential requirements operable and keep them aligned with technological and market developments.
trigger_conditions: A requirement by its nature cannot produce its intended effect unless further specified in a binding Union act, and specification is needed to reflect developments.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Need finding for eligible requirement AND Article 45 procedure; specification must supplement rather than replace the legislative framework.
exceptions_derogations: No binding further specification may be inferred from guidance, a draft or a non-effective delegated act.
required_information: Requirement selected; inability to achieve intended effect; technological or market evidence; specification; effective date.
required_control: Eligibility finding, delegation validity, expert consultation, EDIB advice, scrutiny and source-status checks.
required_evidence: Need assessment, EDIB advice, adopted act, Article 45 record, publication and entry-into-force evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: Only a valid effective delegated act changes binding specification; absent such an act, paragraph 1 remains the controlling text.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission shall when adopting delegated acts take into account the advice of the EDIB in accordance with Article 42, point (c)(iii).
addressee_legal_role: Commission adopting an Article 33(2) delegated act.
action: Obtain, assess and take account of the EDIB's relevant advice.
object_granularity: Advice record for one Article 33(2) delegated act.
purpose: Integrate EDIB expertise into binding further specification.
trigger_conditions: The Commission is adopting a delegated act under Article 33(2).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Relevant EDIB advice AND documented consideration in adoption.
exceptions_derogations: Taking into account does not compel adoption of the EDIB position, but unexplained disregard is not equivalent to consideration.
required_information: EDIB advice, issues addressed, Commission response and resulting drafting decision.
required_control: Advice provenance, completeness, response matrix and adoption-dossier linkage.
required_evidence: EDIB advice and Commission record demonstrating consideration.
consequence_or_remedy: A failure to take advice into account may affect procedural legality and needs qualified review.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: references of which are published in the Official Journal of the European Union, shall be presumed to be in conformity
addressee_legal_role: Decision-maker assessing an offering participant's Article 33 conformity.
action: Recognise the participant's presumption only to the extent of OJ-cited standard coverage.
object_granularity: Cited standard part mapped to a specific Article 33(1) requirement.
purpose: Provide a legally bounded standards-based route to presumed conformity.
trigger_conditions: Participant's offering meets a harmonised standard or part; its reference is published in the Official Journal; the relevant requirement is covered.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Meeting standard AND qualifying OJ citation AND requirement coverage; presumption is limited to covered parts.
exceptions_derogations: Drafts, European Standards without qualifying citation, technical reports and uncovered requirements do not activate the presumption.
required_information: Standard identifier and version; OJ reference; covered clauses; requirement-coverage mapping; evidence of meeting the standard.
required_form_or_format: Official Journal citation plus traceable conformance and coverage evidence.
required_control: Formal-status, OJ-citation, version, scope and coverage verification.
required_evidence: Official Journal citation, standard conformance evidence and clause-to-requirement matrix.
consequence_or_remedy: Presumption applies only to covered requirements; all other requirements remain independently assessable.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission shall, pursuant to Article 10 of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012, request one or more European standardisation organisations to draft harmonised standards
addressee_legal_role: Commission authority issuing an Article 10 standardisation request.
action: Request harmonised standards satisfying Article 33(1) essential requirements.
object_granularity: Commission request and the requirement coverage assigned to standardisation organisations.
purpose: Develop a harmonised-standards route for Article 33 interoperability.
trigger_conditions: Article 33(4) mandate; execution follows Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012 Article 10.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Request one OR more recognised European standardisation organisations; request must target paragraph 1 requirements.
exceptions_derogations: A request or accepted work programme is not itself a harmonised standard and creates no Article 33(3) presumption.
required_information: Requested requirements, deliverables, deadline, recognised organisations and acceptance status.
required_form_or_format: Formal standardisation request under Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012 Article 10.
required_control: Request legal-basis, acceptance, delivery, compliance and OJ-citation status register.
required_evidence: Commission request, acceptance evidence, work programme and delivery records.
consequence_or_remedy: Failure outcomes may contribute to the Article 33(5) fallback triggers, but those triggers require separate cumulative assessment.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission may, by means of implementing acts, adopt common specifications covering any or all of the essential requirements
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority for the exceptional common-specification route.
action: Adopt a common specification covering some or all Article 33(1) requirements.
object_granularity: Implementing act, specification part and covered essential requirement.
purpose: Provide a binding fallback when the harmonised-standard route has failed or is unavailable under the statutory conditions.
trigger_conditions: One Article 33(5)(a)(i)-(iii) standardisation failure AND Article 33(5)(b) no-citation/no-reasonable-expectation condition.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: [(a)(i) OR (a)(ii) OR (a)(iii)] AND (b); adoption remains discretionary and uses the examination procedure.
exceptions_derogations: Common specifications are an exceptional fallback; accepted standardisation work or mere absence of a current citation does not alone prove the full trigger.
required_information: Request status, delivery deadline, delivered-standard compliance, OJ-citation status, reasonable-period expectation and requirements covered.
required_form_or_format: Commission implementing act containing common specifications.
required_control: Cumulative-trigger decision tree, evidence date, consultation and examination-procedure checklist.
required_evidence: Standardisation-request file, OJ search, expectation assessment, consultation dossier, committee record and adopted act.
consequence_or_remedy: Only an adopted applicable act creates a common specification or supports the paragraph 8 presumption.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the request has not been accepted
addressee_legal_role: Commission assessing availability of the common-specification fallback.
action: The relevant standardisation request has not been accepted.
object_granularity: Relevant request and requirement coverage.
purpose: Bound the fallback route to a verified request-acceptance failure.
trigger_conditions: A relevant Article 10(1) request was made and its acceptance status is assessed.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: This limb is alternative to (a)(ii) and (a)(iii), but any selected limb remains cumulative with paragraph 5(b).
exceptions_derogations: M/614 was accepted on 2025-07-07, so the non-acceptance condition was not satisfied for that request at the cut-off. Acceptance does not determine the other alternative failure limbs.
required_information: Request identifier, addressees, acceptance decision and date.
required_control: Authoritative acceptance evidence and requirement-scope matching.
required_evidence: Request and formal acceptance or rejection evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: If this limb and paragraph 5(b) hold, the Commission may consider the common-specification route; no automatic adoption follows.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the harmonised standards addressing that request are not delivered within the deadline
addressee_legal_role: Commission assessing availability of the common-specification fallback.
action: Determine and document whether requested standards were delivered by the applicable deadline.
object_granularity: Each requested deliverable and deadline.
purpose: Bound the fallback route to a verified delivery failure.
trigger_conditions: The request was accepted and its delivery deadline and deliverables are identifiable.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: This limb is alternative to (a)(i) and (a)(iii), but any selected limb remains cumulative with paragraph 5(b).
exceptions_derogations: Before the delivery deadline, non-delivery does not satisfy this limb.
required_information: Request deadline, deliverable list, submission date and completeness.
required_form_or_format: Documented delivery-status finding tied to the formal request.
required_control: Deadline calculation, delivery receipt and scope-completeness check.
required_evidence: Formal request, deadline, delivery or non-delivery record.
consequence_or_remedy: If this limb and paragraph 5(b) hold, the Commission may consider the common-specification route; no automatic adoption follows.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the harmonised standards do not comply with the request
addressee_legal_role: Commission assessing delivered standards and fallback eligibility.
action: The harmonised standards delivered in response to the request do not comply with that request.
object_granularity: Delivered standard or part mapped to the request.
purpose: Bound the fallback route to a verified substantive failure of the standardisation output.
trigger_conditions: A requested standard has been delivered and can be assessed against the request.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: This limb is alternative to (a)(i) and (a)(ii), but any selected limb remains cumulative with paragraph 5(b).
exceptions_derogations: At the 2026-07-17 cut-off M/614 remained under execution and no completed deliverables had been assessed against the request, so this condition was not applicable.
required_information: Request requirements, delivered clauses, coverage, deviations and assessment conclusion.
required_control: Version integrity, requirement traceability and reasoned assessment approval.
required_evidence: Delivered standard, formal request and Commission assessment.
consequence_or_remedy: If this limb and paragraph 5(b) hold, the Commission may consider the common-specification route; no automatic adoption follows.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: no reference to harmonised standards covering the relevant essential requirements laid down in paragraph 1 of this Article is published in the Official Journal
addressee_legal_role: Commission assessing availability of the common-specification fallback.
action: No reference to a harmonised standard covering the relevant Article 33(1) requirements has been published in the Official Journal and no such reference is expected within a reasonable period.
object_granularity: Relevant essential requirement and potential OJ-cited standard coverage.
purpose: Prevent fallback common specifications while a covering harmonised-standard citation exists or is expected within a reasonable period.
trigger_conditions: A specific set of Article 33(1) requirements is proposed for common-specification coverage.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: No covering OJ reference AND no such reference expected within a reasonable period; this paragraph 5(b) limb is also cumulative with one paragraph 5(a) limb.
exceptions_derogations: At the cut-off no covering OJ reference was identified, but M/614 remained under execution and the record did not establish whether a resulting reference was expected within a reasonable period.
required_information: Relevant requirements, OJ search results and documented standardisation and expected-citation status.
required_form_or_format: Dated OJ evidence and documented status of any expected citation.
required_control: Authoritative OJ search, coverage mapping and current standardisation-status evidence.
required_evidence: OJ search record and documented standardisation and expected-citation status.
consequence_or_remedy: Without both absence and no-reasonable-expectation findings, Article 33(5) common-specification adoption is not available.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 46(2).
addressee_legal_role: Commission implementing authority using the Data Act examination procedure.
action: Use the Article 46(2) examination procedure when adopting common specifications.
object_granularity: Adoption procedure for each Article 33(5) implementing act.
purpose: Subject fallback specifications to the prescribed Member-State committee control.
trigger_conditions: All Article 33(5) triggers are satisfied and the Commission proceeds to adopt a common-specification act.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Article 46(2) incorporation AND Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5 examination steps.
exceptions_derogations: No alternative procedure is provided for an Article 33(5) implementing act.
required_information: Legal basis, committee opinion, voting outcome and adopted act.
required_form_or_format: Examination procedure under Article 46(2) and Regulation (EU) No 182/2011 Article 5.
required_control: Procedure checklist and committee-record verification.
required_evidence: Committee dossier, opinion or voting record, adopted text and publication record.
consequence_or_remedy: A procedural defect can affect validity and requires qualified legal assessment.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the Commission shall inform the committee referred to in Article 22 of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012
addressee_legal_role: Commission preparing a draft common-specification implementing act.
action: Notify the Article 22 committee of the Commission's fallback-condition conclusion before drafting.
object_granularity: Pre-draft notification for one proposed implementing act.
purpose: Coordinate the exceptional fallback with the Union standardisation process.
trigger_conditions: The Commission intends to prepare an Article 33(5) draft and considers paragraph 5 conditions fulfilled.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Commission conclusion on all paragraph 5 conditions AND committee notification BEFORE draft preparation.
exceptions_derogations: Informal consultation after drafting does not satisfy the express sequencing requirement.
required_information: Proposed requirements, trigger assessment, notification date and committee recipient.
required_form_or_format: Documented notice to the Article 22 committee.
required_control: Sequence check, recipient identity and trigger-assessment attachment.
required_evidence: Dated notification, delivery evidence and underlying paragraph 5 assessment.
consequence_or_remedy: Missing or late notice may affect procedural legality and requires qualified review.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the Commission shall take into account the advice of the EDIB and views of other relevant bodies or expert groups
addressee_legal_role: Commission preparing a draft Article 33(5) common-specification implementing act.
action: Take account of the specified EDIB advice and other relevant expert or institutional views.
object_granularity: Advice and views relating to one draft implementing act.
purpose: Ensure relevant institutional and technical expertise informs fallback common specifications.
trigger_conditions: The Commission prepares a draft implementing act under Article 33(5).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: EDIB advice considered AND views of other relevant bodies or expert groups considered.
exceptions_derogations: The distinct duty to consult all relevant stakeholders is recorded in REQ-DATA-033-024.
required_information: Draft identifier, EDIB advice, relevant-body or expert-group views and available evidence that they were considered.
required_form_or_format: No documentary form or prescribed weight or outcome is specified by Article 33(7).
required_control: Link the relevant inputs to the draft-preparation process without treating receipt or listing alone as conclusive.
required_evidence: Available advice, submitted views, meeting records, draft history or other evidence showing consideration.
consequence_or_remedy: Failure to take required inputs into account may affect procedural legality and requires qualified review.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: meet the common specifications established by implementing acts referred to in paragraph 5 or parts thereof shall be presumed to be in conformity
addressee_legal_role: Decision-maker assessing an offering participant's Article 33 conformity.
action: A participant whose offering meets an applicable common specification is presumed to conform only with the Article 33(1) requirements covered by that specification or the relevant part thereof.
object_granularity: Common-specification part mapped to a specific Article 33(1) requirement.
purpose: Provide a legally bounded common-specification route to presumed conformity.
trigger_conditions: A valid Article 33(5) act establishes the specification; the offering meets it; the asserted requirement is covered.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Applicable adopted act AND meeting specification AND requirement coverage; presumption limited to covered parts.
exceptions_derogations: A standardisation request, draft, non-Article 33 specification and uncovered requirement does not activate the presumption.
required_information: Implementing-act identifier; applicable version; covered clauses; requirement map; evidence of meeting the specification.
required_form_or_format: Adopted implementing act plus traceable conformance and coverage evidence.
required_control: Formal-status, applicability, version, scope and coverage verification.
required_evidence: Adopted act, conformance evidence and clause-to-requirement matrix.
consequence_or_remedy: Presumption applies only to covered requirements; all other requirements remain independently assessable.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the Commission shall repeal the implementing acts referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article, or parts thereof
addressee_legal_role: Commission maintaining common-specification implementing acts after harmonised-standard citation.
action: Repeal the Article 33(5) implementing acts or parts that cover the same essential requirements as the cited harmonised standard.
object_granularity: Each implementing act or part overlapping the cited standard's essential-requirement coverage.
purpose: Remove overlapping fallback specifications after the harmonised-standard route becomes operative.
trigger_conditions: The harmonised standard's reference is published in the Official Journal of the European Union.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Qualifying Official Journal publication AND same-essential-requirement overlap AND repeal to the extent of that overlap.
exceptions_derogations: No repeal follows from a proposal or assessment alone; non-overlapping act provisions remain unaffected.
required_information: Official Journal reference, standard scope, essential-requirement coverage, affected implementing acts and overlapping parts.
required_form_or_format: Formal repeal of the overlapping implementing act provisions.
required_control: Verify publication, legal identity, version, clause-to-requirement overlap, repeal scope and temporal sequence.
required_evidence: Official Journal citation, coverage matrix, affected-act inventory and repeal act.
consequence_or_remedy: Overlapping common-specification provisions must be repealed; remaining provisions and prior-period effects require transition analysis.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: it shall inform the Commission thereof by submitting a detailed explanation.
addressee_legal_role: Member State evaluating whether an adopted Article 33 common specification entirely satisfies the essential requirements.
action: Inform the Commission by submitting a detailed explanation.
object_granularity: The challenged common specification and the Article 33(1) requirements alleged not to be entirely satisfied.
purpose: Bring a potentially insufficient common specification to the Commission for formal assessment.
trigger_conditions: A Member State considers that a common specification does not entirely satisfy the essential requirements in Article 33(1).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Member-State insufficiency conclusion AND Commission notification AND a detailed explanation.
exceptions_derogations: The notification duty does not arise without the Member State's insufficiency conclusion.
required_information: Identified common specification, affected Article 33(1) requirements, reasons for insufficiency and supporting facts.
required_form_or_format: Submission to the Commission in the form of a detailed explanation.
required_control: Verify Member-State authority, specification identity, requirement coverage, explanation completeness and transmission.
required_evidence: Dated notification, detailed explanation, supporting material and proof of submission.
consequence_or_remedy: A qualifying notification triggers the Commission assessment duty recorded in REQ-DATA-033-022.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission may adopt guidelines taking into account the proposal of the EDIB in accordance with Article 30, point (h), of Regulation (EU) 2022/868
addressee_legal_role: Commission guidance authority under Article 33(11).
action: Adopt guidelines laying down interoperable frameworks for common data-space standards and practices.
object_granularity: Guideline instrument, framework or addressed data-space practice.
purpose: Support coherent functioning of common European data spaces.
trigger_conditions: The Commission elects to act and an EDIB proposal under Regulation (EU) 2022/868 Article 30(h) is available for consideration.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Guidelines are discretionary; EDIB proposal must be taken into account; framework concerns common standards and practices.
exceptions_derogations: Guidelines do not themselves replace Article 33 binding requirements, delegated acts, common specifications or OJ-cited standards.
required_information: EDIB proposal, framework scope, common standards and practices, target data spaces and legal-status statement.
required_control: Proposal provenance, non-binding-status clarity, scope and consistency with binding law.
required_evidence: EDIB proposal, adopted guideline and publication record.
consequence_or_remedy: Guidelines may inform implementation but cannot alone create a conformity presumption or displace binding law.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: The Commission shall assess that detailed explanation
addressee_legal_role: Commission authority receiving and assessing a Member State's Article 33(10) explanation.
action: Assess the detailed explanation.
object_granularity: The Member-State explanation and the challenged common specification's coverage of Article 33(1) requirements.
purpose: Determine whether the challenged common specification entirely satisfies the applicable essential requirements.
trigger_conditions: The Commission receives the detailed explanation submitted under Article 33(10).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Qualifying Member-State notification AND detailed explanation AND Commission assessment.
exceptions_derogations: No automatic amendment follows merely from receipt; the assessment duty is distinct from the discretionary power in REQ-DATA-033-023.
required_information: Member-State explanation, challenged specification, applicable Article 33(1) requirements and supporting material.
required_form_or_format: A documented Commission assessment adequate to support the ensuing appropriateness decision.
required_control: Verify receipt, completeness, relevant requirement coverage, evidence evaluation and reasoned conclusion.
required_evidence: Submitted explanation, assessment record, supporting evidence and conclusion.
consequence_or_remedy: The assessment informs whether the Commission may amend the establishing implementing act under REQ-DATA-033-023.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: may, if appropriate, amend the implementing act establishing the common specification in question.
addressee_legal_role: Commission authority deciding whether to amend the implementing act establishing the challenged common specification.
action: Amend the implementing act establishing the common specification where appropriate.
object_granularity: The establishing implementing act or affected part of the challenged common specification.
purpose: Correct a common specification when the Commission's assessment supports amendment.
trigger_conditions: The Commission has assessed the detailed explanation and considers amendment appropriate.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Completed assessment AND appropriateness finding; exercise of the amendment power remains discretionary.
exceptions_derogations: Article 33(10) creates no automatic amendment duty when amendment is not considered appropriate.
required_information: Assessment findings, affected implementing-act provisions, revised specification content and reasons for amendment.
required_form_or_format: An amending implementing act following the applicable Article 33(5) procedure.
required_control: Verify assessment completion, legal basis, appropriateness reasoning, procedure, scope, versioning and publication.
required_evidence: Assessment record, appropriateness decision, draft and adopted amending act, and publication record.
consequence_or_remedy: An adopted amendment changes the applicable common specification; a decision not to amend leaves the existing act in force subject to other lawful action.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: shall duly consult all relevant stakeholders.
addressee_legal_role: Commission preparing and consulting on a draft Article 33(5) common-specification implementing act.
action: Duly consult all relevant stakeholders.
object_granularity: Stakeholder consultation process for one draft implementing act.
purpose: Ensure affected interests and implementation realities inform fallback common specifications.
trigger_conditions: The Commission prepares a draft implementing act under Article 33(5).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: All relevant stakeholders identified AND given a due opportunity to participate in the consultation.
exceptions_derogations: No express exception; relevance and due consultation remain context-sensitive terms.
required_information: Draft identifier, relevant-stakeholder criteria, stakeholder population, consultation method, notice, timing and participation records.
required_control: Verify relevance criteria, stakeholder coverage, accessibility, timing, participation evidence and draft linkage.
required_evidence: Stakeholder map, consultation notice, submissions or meeting records, and draft history.
consequence_or_remedy: Inadequate consultation may affect procedural legality and requires qualified review.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the Commission shall assess the harmonised standard in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012.
addressee_legal_role: Commission assessing a harmonised standard proposed for Official Journal citation.
action: Assess the proposed harmonised standard in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012.
object_granularity: One adopted harmonised standard proposed to the Commission for Official Journal citation.

required_information: Standard text and version, standardisation request, proposal, essential-requirement coverage and Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012 assessment criteria.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: shall be developed on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5
addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for development of the high-risk AI system.

addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for the system's dataset governance.

addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for the system's dataset governance.

consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for dataset preparation and governance.

required_control: Pipeline logging, approval, reproducibility and quality-impact review.
required_evidence: Transformation logs, code or configuration, dataset lineage and validation results.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the formulation of assumptions, in particular with respect to the information that the data are supposed to measure and represent
addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for dataset and model assumptions.

purpose: Expose construct, measurement and representation choices that affect validity and bias.
