cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Identify assumptions and connect them to what data are supposed to measure and represent.
exceptions_derogations: Unstated assumptions are not absent assumptions; uncertainty must be recorded where verification is impossible.
required_information: Assumption, construct, proxy, represented population, limitations, evidence and review outcome.
required_form_or_format: Documented assumption register linked to technical documentation.
required_control: Assumption review, validity evidence, limitation tracking and change control.
required_evidence: Measurement specification, labelling guidance, validation study and assumption record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: an assessment of the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed
addressee_legal_role: Provider planning and approving datasets for development and testing.
action: Assess needed datasets for availability, quantity and suitability.
object_granularity: Required dataset, dataset role or coverage stratum.
purpose: Ensure dataset planning is adequate for intended purpose and risk.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(2) applies and dataset needs are being defined or revised.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Assess availability AND quantity AND suitability for every needed dataset role.
exceptions_derogations: The provision does not prescribe a universal minimum quantity; inadequacy or gaps flow into Article 10(2)(h).
required_information: Needed role, target population, quantity, availability, suitability criteria, shortfall and mitigation.
required_control: Needs baseline, quantitative analysis, suitability approval and gap escalation.
required_evidence: Data plan, power or coverage analysis where appropriate, source inventory and assessment record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: examination in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights
addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for dataset bias examination.
action: Examine datasets and data flows for legally relevant possible biases.
object_granularity: Bias hypothesis, affected dataset or group, harm pathway and feedback loop.
purpose: Identify bias risks to health, safety, fundamental rights and non-discrimination before or during development.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(2) applies and a possible bias could be likely to cause one of the listed adverse effects.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Examine health/safety harm OR fundamental-rights impact OR prohibited discrimination; specifically consider outputs influencing future inputs.
exceptions_derogations: Not every statistical difference is a legally relevant bias; the likely-effect and protected-interest links require analysis.
required_information: Bias hypothesis, affected group, metric, causal pathway, likelihood, legal interest, feedback mechanism and finding.
required_form_or_format: Documented bias examination linked to risk management and technical documentation.
required_control: Multidisciplinary review, subgroup analysis, feedback-loop test, limitation record and escalation.
required_evidence: Bias analysis, dataset statistics, domain review, rights-impact evidence and decision record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases identified according to point (f)
addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for responding to identified bias risks.
action: Design, implement and verify appropriate bias detection, prevention and mitigation measures.
object_granularity: One identified bias and its control set.
purpose: Reduce legally relevant bias risks identified through the required examination.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(2)(f) examination identifies a possible bias requiring treatment.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Measures address detection, prevention and mitigation as appropriate to the identified risk; effectiveness must be assessed.
exceptions_derogations: Appropriate does not require an impossible guarantee of zero bias, but unexplained inaction is not a measure.
required_information: Identified bias, selected measure, owner, implementation date, metric, residual risk and review result.
required_form_or_format: Documented technical or organisational control with effectiveness evidence.
required_control: Control selection, testing, residual-risk acceptance, monitoring and corrective-action loop.
required_evidence: Mitigation plan, implementation record, test results and residual-risk decision.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the identification of relevant data gaps or shortcomings that prevent compliance with this Regulation, and how those gaps and shortcomings can be addressed
addressee_legal_role: Provider responsible for dataset compliance gaps.
action: Identify compliance-blocking data gaps or shortcomings and define responses.
object_granularity: One gap or shortcoming, affected requirement and response.
purpose: Prevent unresolved data deficiencies from undermining regulatory compliance.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(2) applies and a data gap or shortcoming may prevent compliance.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Identify relevant gaps or shortcomings AND document how each can be addressed.
exceptions_derogations: Recording a gap does not itself establish compliance; unresolved blocking gaps must remain visible.
required_information: Gap, affected requirement, cause, materiality, owner, response, deadline, residual limitation and status.
required_control: Requirement linkage, severity triage, owner and deadline, closure evidence and escalation.
required_evidence: Gap analysis, remediation plan, implementation proof and closure decision.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: shall be relevant, sufficiently representative, and to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete
addressee_legal_role: Provider of a high-risk AI system to which Article 10 applies.
action: Ensure the datasets meet the stated relevance, representativity, error and completeness characteristics.
object_granularity: Individual dataset or permitted combination in relation to intended purpose.
purpose: Provide a high-quality data basis for intended-purpose system performance and rights protection.
trigger_conditions: The actor is the provider of a high-risk AI system and training, validation or testing datasets are used within Article 10 scope.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Relevant AND sufficiently representative AND best-extent-possible error freedom AND best-extent-possible completeness; sentence 3 permits individual or combined satisfaction.
exceptions_derogations: No express exception; 'to the best extent possible' qualifies error freedom and completeness, and Article 10(6) narrows dataset coverage for systems not using model-training techniques.
required_information: Intended purpose, relevance evidence, target population, representativity method, error definition/rate, completeness definition/rate and limitations.
required_form_or_format: Documented metrics, methods and reasoned quality finding.
required_control: Quality plan, representative-population review, error/completeness testing and approval.
required_evidence: Quality report, sampling analysis, validation results, issue logs and limitation record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: They shall have the appropriate statistical properties, including, where applicable, as regards the persons or groups of persons
addressee_legal_role: Provider selecting and statistically validating high-risk AI datasets.
action: Assess and ensure appropriate statistical properties for relevant persons or groups.
object_granularity: Dataset or combination, target population and relevant subgroup.
purpose: Align dataset statistics with the intended use population and reduce performance or bias failures.
trigger_conditions: Article 10 datasets are used; person- or group-specific properties apply where the system is intended for them.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Statistical properties appropriate to intended purpose; subgroup properties where applicable.
exceptions_derogations: Where applicable qualifies person/group analysis; it requires a reasoned relevance determination.
required_information: Target population, groups, distributions, sampling, prevalence, statistical metrics, uncertainty and limitations.
required_form_or_format: Documented statistical analysis tied to intended purpose.
required_control: Population definition, subgroup coverage, metric selection, uncertainty analysis and approval.
required_evidence: Statistical report, dataset profile, subgroup analysis and validation results.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Those characteristics of the data sets may be met at the level of individual data sets or at the level of a combination thereof.
addressee_legal_role: Provider defining the evidence unit for Article 10(3) characteristics.
action: Demonstrate paragraph 3 characteristics at individual-dataset or combined-dataset level.
object_granularity: Individual dataset OR identified combination.
purpose: Permit a justified portfolio-level evidence approach without lowering the paragraph 3 characteristics.
trigger_conditions: The provider assesses paragraph 3 characteristics across one or more datasets.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Individual-dataset evidence OR combination-level evidence; the chosen unit must still demonstrate every applicable characteristic.
exceptions_derogations: Combination-level assessment cannot conceal a dataset-specific deficiency material to a role, subgroup or intended use.
required_information: Datasets in the unit, roles, combination logic, characteristic metrics and contribution of each dataset.
required_form_or_format: Explicit evidence-unit declaration and combined assessment.
required_control: Combination membership, role compatibility, aggregation method and material-defect check.
required_evidence: Dataset manifest, combination rationale and paragraph 3 assessment.
consequence_or_remedy: An unsupported combination-level claim does not establish paragraph 3 compliance.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: Data sets shall take into account, to the extent required by the intended purpose, the characteristics or elements
addressee_legal_role: Provider selecting datasets for the intended deployment setting.
action: Reflect setting-specific characteristics or elements in dataset design and evidence.
object_granularity: Dataset or combination in relation to a specific intended setting.
purpose: Reduce mismatch between development data and the environment in which the system is intended to operate.
trigger_conditions: An intended geographical, contextual, behavioural or functional setting has characteristics material to dataset fitness.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Account for each material geographical, contextual, behavioural OR functional characteristic to the extent required by intended purpose.
exceptions_derogations: The extent required qualifier demands a reasoned setting-materiality assessment; it is not a general exemption.
required_information: Intended setting, material characteristics, dataset coverage, mismatch, mitigation and residual limitation.
required_control: Setting inventory, materiality review, coverage test and change trigger.
required_evidence: Use-context specification, dataset profile, external-validation results and limitation record.
consequence_or_remedy: Non-compliance with Article 10 may engage AI Act Article 99(4)(a), subject to authority, transition, facts and the applicable fine calculation.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the providers of such systems may exceptionally process special categories of personal data, subject to appropriate safeguards
addressee_legal_role: Provider of a high-risk AI system seeking to use the exceptional Article 10(5) permission.
action: Process special-category personal data for bias detection and correction within the narrow permission.
object_granularity: Specific processing operation, dataset fields and affected data subjects.
purpose: Enable necessary bias detection and correction while protecting fundamental rights and freedoms.
trigger_conditions: The actor is the provider of a high-risk AI system and the processing is strictly necessary for bias detection and correction under Article 10(2)(f) and (g).
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Bias detection AND correction purpose; strict necessity; appropriate safeguards; all second-subparagraph and points (a)-(f) conditions; independent data-protection-law compliance.
exceptions_derogations: The exceptional permission does not authorise unrelated model training, analytics or convenience processing and applies in addition to the applicable data-protection regime.
required_information: Purpose, strict-necessity rationale, alternative-data analysis, data categories, safeguards, persons, duration and deletion endpoint.
required_form_or_format: Documented authorisation and processing record within the applicable data-protection framework.
required_control: Legal basis, strict necessity, purpose limitation, approval, access, security, reuse, non-transfer, deletion and record checks.
required_evidence: Necessity assessment, privacy and rights safeguards, authorisation, logs, processing record and deletion evidence.
consequence_or_remedy: Processing outside the permission may breach the AI Act and independently applicable data-protection law.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: all the following conditions must be met in order for such processing to occur
addressee_legal_role: Provider relying on the Article 10(5) exceptional processing permission.
action: Verify and maintain all additional Article 10(5)(a)-(f) conditions.
object_granularity: One exceptional processing operation and its full safeguard set.
purpose: Make the exceptional permission cumulative, narrow and independently accountable.
trigger_conditions: The provider proposes or continues Article 10(5) special-category processing.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Applicable GDPR/EUDPR provisions PLUS every point (a), (b), (c), (d), (e) and (f); no point is optional.
exceptions_derogations: Satisfaction of Article 10(5) conditions does not waive independent data-protection obligations.
required_information: Condition-by-condition finding, evidence, owner, validity period and change trigger.
required_form_or_format: Cumulative safeguard checklist with evidence references and approval.
required_control: No-start gate, continuous-monitoring gate and stop-processing trigger.
required_evidence: Approved condition matrix and evidence for each point (a)-(f).
consequence_or_remedy: Failure of any condition removes the Article 10(5) permission for the affected processing.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the bias detection and correction cannot be effectively fulfilled by processing other data, including synthetic or anonymised data
addressee_legal_role: Provider relying on the Article 10(5) exceptional processing permission.
action: Demonstrate that other data cannot effectively fulfil the bias purpose.
object_granularity: Bias objective, proposed special-category fields and candidate alternative dataset.
purpose: Reserve special-category processing for cases where less intrusive data are ineffective.
trigger_conditions: Provider proposes special-category processing for bias detection or correction.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Assess other data, explicitly including synthetic and anonymised data; conclude they cannot effectively fulfil the purpose.
exceptions_derogations: Cost or convenience alone does not establish inability; the effectiveness criterion requires evidence.
required_information: Bias objective, alternatives considered, method, effectiveness criteria, results and reasoned conclusion.
required_control: Alternative-data inventory, test protocol, independent challenge and periodic reassessment.
required_evidence: Comparative analysis, test results and approved necessity conclusion.
consequence_or_remedy: If another data route can effectively fulfil the purpose, the Article 10(5) permission is unavailable.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: subject to technical limitations on the re-use of the personal data, and state-of-the-art security and privacy-preserving measures, including pseudonymisation
addressee_legal_role: Provider controlling the exceptional special-category processing environment.
action: Implement technical reuse limitations and state-of-the-art security and privacy-preserving measures.
object_granularity: Dataset, processing environment and data-flow boundary.
purpose: Reduce reuse, security and privacy risks arising from exceptional processing.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(5) special-category processing occurs.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Technical reuse limitation AND state-of-the-art security AND privacy-preserving measures, including pseudonymisation.
exceptions_derogations: Pseudonymisation is included but does not replace other required measures or convert personal data into non-personal data.
required_information: Reuse rule, security controls, privacy techniques, pseudonymisation method, state-of-the-art basis and residual risk.
required_form_or_format: Enforced technical controls with documented configuration and assessment.
required_control: Purpose-bound access, export restriction, encryption, pseudonymisation, monitoring, testing and state-of-the-art review.
required_evidence: Architecture, configuration, test results, access logs and security/privacy assessment.
consequence_or_remedy: Absent safeguards, the exceptional processing condition fails and processing must not proceed or continue.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: secured, protected, subject to suitable safeguards, including strict controls and documentation of the access, to avoid misuse
addressee_legal_role: Provider controlling access to exceptional-processing data.
action: Secure the data and restrict, document and govern access.
object_granularity: Processing environment, user account and access event.
purpose: Avoid misuse and confine access to accountable authorised persons.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(5) special-category processing occurs.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Security and protection; suitable safeguards; strict access controls; access documentation; authorised persons only; appropriate confidentiality obligations.
exceptions_derogations: Technical access alone is insufficient without authorisation and confidentiality; organisational policy alone is insufficient without enforced controls.
required_information: Authorised person, role, purpose, approval, access event, data scope, confidentiality obligation and anomaly.
required_form_or_format: Enforced identity and access controls plus immutable or protected access documentation.
required_control: Least privilege, approval, authentication, logging, review, revocation, misuse detection and confidentiality management.
required_evidence: Authorisation records, access-control configuration, logs, reviews and confidentiality commitments.
consequence_or_remedy: Unauthorised or undocumented access defeats the safeguard condition and may trigger AI, data-protection and security responses.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: the special categories of personal data are not to be transmitted, transferred or otherwise accessed by other parties
addressee_legal_role: Provider controlling exceptional special-category data.
action: Prevent transmission, transfer or other access by outside parties.
object_granularity: Data record, export, transfer path or access channel.
purpose: Contain the data within the authorised exceptional-processing boundary.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(5) special-category processing occurs.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: No transmission; no transfer; no other-party access.
exceptions_derogations: No express Article 10(5)(d) onward-transfer exception is stated; who counts as another party requires role and processing-boundary analysis.
required_information: Approved processing boundary, authorised persons, network paths, exports, recipients and blocked events.
required_control: Network egress, export, sharing, account, vendor and remote-access restrictions with monitoring.
required_evidence: Data-flow map, control configuration, logs and incident records.
consequence_or_remedy: An unauthorised transfer or access breaches the Article 10(5) condition and may trigger additional data-protection consequences.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: are deleted once the bias has been corrected or the personal data has reached the end of its retention period, whichever comes first
addressee_legal_role: Provider retaining special-category data for Article 10(5) processing.
action: Delete the special-category data at the first controlling endpoint.
object_granularity: Affected data record, dataset copy, backup or processing environment.
purpose: Minimise retention after the exceptional bias purpose or lawful retention period ends.
trigger_conditions: Bias is corrected OR the applicable retention period ends.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Two alternative trigger events; delete at whichever occurs first and cover active copies and governed residual copies.
exceptions_derogations: The applicable retention period comes from controlling law or policy; Article 10(5)(e) does not authorise extension beyond bias correction.
required_information: Bias-correction event, retention rule and end date, copies, deletion date, method and exceptions under controlling law.
required_form_or_format: Documented and verifiable deletion across governed storage locations.
required_control: Event monitoring, earliest-date calculation, deletion workflow, backup propagation and exception approval.
required_evidence: Trigger record, retention schedule, deletion logs and verification result.
consequence_or_remedy: Data retained beyond the earlier endpoint fall outside the Article 10(5) condition and require immediate legal response.

source_excerpt_max_25_words: include the reasons why the processing of special categories of personal data was strictly necessary to detect and correct biases
addressee_legal_role: Provider subject to the applicable processing-activity record regime.
action: Record the strict-necessity and alternative-data rationale in the processing record.
object_granularity: One exceptional processing activity and its recorded rationale.
purpose: Make the exceptional permission auditable under AI and data-protection law.
trigger_conditions: Article 10(5) special-category processing occurs and the applicable GDPR or EUDPR processing record is maintained.
cumulative_or_alternative_conditions: Record why processing was strictly necessary AND why other data could not achieve bias detection/correction.
exceptions_derogations: A generic template statement without operation-specific reasons does not demonstrate the required rationale.
required_information: Bias objective, strict-necessity reasons, alternatives considered, effectiveness evidence and approving actor.
required_form_or_format: Record of processing activities under the applicable data-protection instrument.
required_control: Record completeness, operation linkage, approval, versioning and consistency with the alternatives assessment.
required_evidence: Processing record and supporting necessity/alternatives analysis.
consequence_or_remedy: An absent or inadequate rationale breaches the evidence condition and undermines reliance on the exceptional permission.
